ATTORNEY-REVIEW DRAFT – NOT FOR SIGNATURE

This document is a preliminary working draft prepared by an AI assistant for discussion purposes only. It is not legal advice and may be inaccurate, incomplete, or unsuitable for your specific circumstances. A qualified Pakistan-licensed attorney must review, revise, and approve this entire document (including all placeholders) before it is shared with any candidate or relied upon in any way.

---

[Analytics Dojo SMC Private Letterhead]

[Date]

Sara Khan  
[Address Line 1]  
[Address Line 2]  
[City], [Postal Code]  
Pakistan

Subject: Offer of Employment – Data Analyst

Dear Sara Khan,

Analytics Dojo SMC Private ("ADS", "we", or "the Company") is pleased to offer you employment in the position of **Data Analyst** on the terms and conditions set out in this offer letter (the "Offer Letter"). This Offer Letter is issued in accordance with ADS’s standard employment offer structure and fixed defaults for Pakistan-based employees, subject always to mandatory Pakistan labour law.

## 1. Position and Reporting

1.1 **Position.** You will be employed as a **Data Analyst** (the "Position"). Your primary duties will include data analysis, reporting, dashboarding, and such other related responsibilities as may reasonably be assigned to you from time to time, consistent with this Position and ADS’s business needs.

1.2 **Reporting Line.** You will report to **[BLANK – Reporting Manager Title/Name]** or such other person as the Company may designate from time to time.

1.3 **Work Location.** Your primary work location will be **[BLANK – City/Office or Remote within Pakistan]**, subject to reasonable travel within or outside Pakistan as may be required for business purposes, in accordance with applicable law.

1.4 **Full-Time Role.** This is a full-time position. During your employment, you are expected to devote your full working time and attention to the business of the Company and not to engage in any other employment, consultancy, or business that conflicts with your duties to the Company, except with the Company’s prior written consent and subject to applicable law.

## 2. Start Date and Term

2.1 **Start Date.** Your employment with the Company is expected to commence on **[BLANK – Start Date]** (the "Start Date"), or such other date as may be mutually agreed in writing.

2.2 **Term.** Your employment is intended to be of an ongoing, indefinite duration, subject to termination by either party in accordance with this Offer Letter and applicable Pakistan labour law.

## 3. Compensation and Benefits

3.1 **Base Salary (ADS Fixed Defaults Applied Where Applicable).**

(a) Your gross base salary will be **[BLANK – Annual or Monthly Gross Salary in PKR]**, payable in arrears in **[BLANK – Monthly/Other Frequency]** instalments, less applicable deductions and withholdings as required by Pakistan law.  
(b) ADS’s standard Pakistan payroll practices, pay cycle, and statutory deductions (including income tax withholding and social security contributions, if applicable) will apply as fixed defaults.

3.2 **Bonuses and Variable Compensation.**

(a) You may be eligible to participate in ADS’s discretionary bonus or incentive schemes, if any, as may be in effect from time to time for similarly situated employees in Pakistan, subject to the terms of such schemes and the Company’s sole discretion.  
(b) Any bonus or variable compensation is not guaranteed unless expressly confirmed in writing and is not part of your base salary.

3.3 **Benefits (ADS Fixed Defaults for Pakistan).**

Subject to eligibility and the terms of the relevant policies and plans as amended from time to time, you will be entitled to participate in ADS’s standard benefits for Pakistan-based employees, which currently include (subject to mandatory law):

(a) **Statutory benefits** required under Pakistan labour law, including but not limited to applicable social security, EOBI (if applicable), and other mandatory schemes.  
(b) **Annual leave, sick leave, and public holidays** in accordance with Pakistan labour law and ADS’s standard leave policy for Pakistan.  
(c) **Health or medical coverage** and any other benefits that ADS may offer from time to time to employees in similar positions in Pakistan, as per ADS’s fixed default benefits framework.

The Company reserves the right to modify, replace, or discontinue any non-statutory benefit or plan at its discretion, subject to applicable law.

3.4 **Deductions.** The Company will make all deductions from your compensation as required by Pakistan law and any additional deductions authorised by you in writing, to the extent permitted by law.

## 4. Working Hours and Leave

4.1 **Working Hours.** Your normal working hours will be **[BLANK – Standard Working Hours, e.g., 9:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m., Monday to Friday]**, subject to ADS’s standard Pakistan working-hours policy and any reasonable adjustments required by business needs, in compliance with applicable labour laws on maximum hours and overtime.

4.2 **Overtime.** If you are eligible for overtime under Pakistan labour law, overtime will be compensated in accordance with applicable law and ADS’s standard overtime policy for Pakistan.

4.3 **Leave Entitlements.** You will be entitled to annual leave, casual leave, sick leave, and public holidays in accordance with Pakistan labour law and ADS’s standard leave policy for Pakistan-based employees. Details of your leave entitlements will be provided in the employee handbook or relevant policy documents.

## 5. Probation and Confirmation

5.1 **Probation Period (ADS Fixed Default).** Your employment will initially be subject to a probationary period of **[BLANK – Probation Duration, e.g., three (3) or six (6) months]** commencing from the Start Date, in line with ADS’s standard Pakistan probation policy and subject to Pakistan labour law.

5.2 **Confirmation.** Upon successful completion of the probation period, and subject to satisfactory performance and conduct, your employment may be confirmed in writing. The Company may, in its discretion and in accordance with applicable law, extend the probation period or decide not to confirm your employment.

## 6. Duties, Conduct, and Policies

6.1 **Duties.** You agree to perform your duties diligently, faithfully, and to the best of your abilities, and to comply with all lawful and reasonable instructions of the Company.

6.2 **Policies and Procedures (ADS Fixed Defaults).** You agree to comply with all Company policies, procedures, codes of conduct, and employee handbooks applicable to your role and location, as may be amended from time to time, including but not limited to:

- Code of Conduct and Ethics;  
- Anti-harassment and anti-discrimination policies;  
- Information security and acceptable use policies;  
- Data protection and privacy policies;  
- Health, safety, and environment policies; and  
- Any other ADS global or Pakistan-specific policies notified to you.

In the event of any conflict between a Company policy and mandatory Pakistan labour law, the mandatory law will prevail.

## 7. Confidentiality, Intellectual Property, and NNN Obligations

7.1 **Confidentiality.** You acknowledge that during your employment you will have access to confidential and proprietary information of the Company and its clients. You agree to keep all such information strictly confidential and not to disclose or use it except as required for the proper performance of your duties, both during and after your employment, in accordance with ADS’s standard confidentiality and non-disclosure obligations.

7.2 **Intellectual Property.** All intellectual property, including but not limited to inventions, discoveries, works of authorship, software, data models, analyses, reports, and other materials that you create, develop, or contribute to in the course of your employment (whether alone or jointly with others) and that relate to the business of the Company shall be the exclusive property of the Company, to the fullest extent permitted by Pakistan law. You agree to execute any documents and do all acts reasonably required to vest such rights in the Company.

7.3 **NNN (Non-Disclosure, Non-Use, Non-Circumvention) Maintenance and Flow-Down.**

(a) You agree to comply with ADS’s standard NNN obligations, including non-disclosure, non-use, and non-circumvention of the Company’s and its clients’ confidential information and business opportunities.  
(b) Where ADS has contractual NNN or similar obligations to its clients or partners, you agree that such obligations may be flowed down to you as an employee to the extent relevant to your work, and you will comply with such obligations as notified to you.  
(c) You acknowledge that breach of NNN obligations may result in disciplinary action up to and including termination, and may expose you to legal liability under applicable law.

## 8. Outside Activities and Conflicts of Interest

8.1 **No Conflicting Engagements.** During your employment, you will not, without the Company’s prior written consent, engage in any other employment, consultancy, or business that conflicts or is likely to conflict with the interests of the Company or your duties.

8.2 **Disclosure of Conflicts.** You agree to promptly disclose to the Company any actual or potential conflict of interest that may arise during your employment.

## 9. Data Protection and Privacy

9.1 **Personal Data.** The Company will process your personal data in accordance with applicable data protection laws and ADS’s data protection and privacy policies, as may be updated from time to time.

9.2 **Consent.** By accepting this Offer Letter, you consent (to the extent required by law) to the collection, use, transfer, and processing of your personal data by the Company and its affiliates for employment, payroll, benefits administration, compliance, and related purposes, subject to applicable Pakistan data protection requirements.

## 10. Termination

10.1 **Termination by Either Party (Pakistan Labour Law Compliant).**

(a) Either you or the Company may terminate your employment by giving **[BLANK – Notice Period, e.g., thirty (30) days]** prior written notice or payment in lieu of notice, where permitted by Pakistan labour law.  
(b) Any notice or payment in lieu of notice will be provided in accordance with applicable Pakistan labour law and ADS’s standard Pakistan termination practices.

10.2 **Termination During Probation.** During the probation period, either party may terminate the employment relationship by giving **[BLANK – Probationary Notice Period, if any]** written notice or payment in lieu of notice, if permitted by applicable law, subject to minimum statutory requirements.

10.3 **Termination for Cause.** The Company may terminate your employment without notice or with shorter notice (if permitted by law) for cause, including but not limited to serious misconduct, gross negligence, dishonesty, breach of confidentiality or NNN obligations, or any other ground recognised under Pakistan labour law and Company policy.

10.4 **Final Settlement.** Upon termination of your employment for any reason, the Company will pay you all salary and benefits due up to your last working day, together with any statutory end-of-service or other payments required under Pakistan labour law, subject to lawful deductions.

## 11. Return of Company Property

Upon termination of your employment for any reason, or upon the Company’s earlier request, you will promptly return to the Company all Company property in your possession or control, including but not limited to laptops, devices, documents, data, access cards, and any copies thereof, whether in physical or electronic form.

## 12. Pakistan Labour Law Compliance Table (Pipeline G – Jurisdiction-Specific Checklist)

The following table summarises how this Offer Letter is intended to align with key Pakistan labour law and ADS jurisdiction-specific checklist items. This table is for convenience only and does not replace legal advice. In case of conflict, mandatory Pakistan law prevails.

| Topic / Checklist Item                                   | Pakistan / ADS Treatment in this Offer Letter |
|----------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|
| **Employment Type**                                      | Indefinite-term employment, subject to termination in accordance with Pakistan labour law (Sections 2.2, 10). |
| **Notice and Termination**                               | Notice periods and termination rights left as [BLANK] placeholders to be set at or above Pakistan statutory minimums; termination for cause aligned with recognised grounds under Pakistan law (Section 10). |
| **Probation**                                            | Probation period included as an ADS fixed default, with duration left as [BLANK] to be set within Pakistan-legal limits (Section 5). |
| **Working Hours and Overtime**                           | Working hours and overtime referenced as subject to Pakistan labour law and ADS standard policy; specific hours left as [BLANK] (Section 4). |
| **Wages / Prompt Payment**                               | Salary payable in arrears on ADS’s standard Pakistan payroll cycle, with statutory deductions; intended to comply with Pakistan wage payment timing requirements (Section 3.1, 3.4). |
| **Prevailing Wage / Minimum Wage**                       | Compensation to be set at or above applicable Pakistan minimum wage and any sectoral requirements; amount left as [BLANK] to be confirmed by HR/legal (Section 3.1). |
| **Benefits and Statutory Contributions**                 | Express reference to statutory benefits (social security, EOBI, etc.) and compliance with Pakistan labour law; non-statutory benefits are discretionary (Section 3.3). |
| **Anti-Indemnity / Indemnity Limitations**               | No employee indemnity obligations imposed on you; this Offer Letter does not require you to indemnify the Company or third parties, consistent with employee-protection norms. |
| **Retainage / Withholding Beyond Lawful Deductions**     | Only lawful deductions and employee-authorised deductions are permitted; no construction-style retainage or unlawful withholding (Section 3.4). |
| **Flow-Down Obligations (Client / NNN / IP)**            | Flow-down of client NNN and confidentiality obligations expressly acknowledged, limited to what is relevant to your work (Section 7.3). |
| **NNN Maintenance (Non-Disclosure/Non-Use/Non-Circumv.)**| NNN obligations explicitly stated as part of confidentiality and IP framework; breach may lead to discipline and legal action (Section 7). |
| **Governing Law and Jurisdiction**                       | Pakistan law and local courts specified as governing law and forum (Section 13). |
| **At-Will vs. Local Law**                                | No at-will language; termination framed strictly in accordance with Pakistan labour law (Section 10). |
| **Ratification / Entire Agreement**                      | Entire agreement and ratification clause included to confirm that this Offer Letter supersedes prior understandings, subject to mandatory law (Section 14). |

## 13. Governing Law and Dispute Resolution

13.1 **Governing Law.** This Offer Letter and any non-contractual obligations arising out of or in connection with it shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, subject always to mandatory labour law protections.

13.2 **Jurisdiction.** Subject to any mandatory dispute resolution mechanisms under Pakistan labour law (including labour courts or tribunals), the courts of Pakistan shall have exclusive jurisdiction over any disputes arising out of or in connection with this Offer Letter or your employment.

## 14. Entire Agreement, Amendments, and Ratification

14.1 **Entire Agreement.** This Offer Letter, together with any referenced policies and any separate confidentiality, IP, or NNN agreements that you may be required to sign, constitutes the entire understanding between you and the Company with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous oral or written communications, representations, or understandings relating to your employment offer, except where Pakistan labour law requires otherwise.

14.2 **Amendments.** Any amendment or modification to this Offer Letter must be in writing and signed by an authorised representative of the Company and, where required by law, by you.

14.3 **Ratification of ADS Fixed Defaults.** By signing this Offer Letter, you acknowledge that:

(a) ADS’s standard Pakistan employment defaults (including probation, benefits framework, working-hours framework, and policy framework) apply to your employment, except where expressly varied in this Offer Letter or required to be modified by Pakistan labour law; and  
(b) In the event of any inconsistency between ADS fixed defaults and mandatory Pakistan labour law, the mandatory law will prevail, and the inconsistent default will be deemed modified to the minimum extent necessary to comply with law.

14.4 **Severability.** If any provision of this Offer Letter is held to be invalid or unenforceable under applicable law, such provision shall be deemed modified to the minimum extent necessary to make it valid and enforceable, and the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect.

## 15. Acceptance of Offer

Please indicate your acceptance of this Offer Letter and the terms and conditions of employment set out above by signing and dating the acknowledgement below and returning a signed copy to **[BLANK – ADS Contact/HR Email]** by **[BLANK – Offer Expiry Date]**, unless extended in writing by the Company.

This offer is conditional upon:

(a) Your confirmation that you are legally entitled to work in Pakistan and will maintain such entitlement throughout your employment; and  
(b) Completion of any background checks or verifications that ADS may reasonably require, in compliance with applicable law.

We look forward to welcoming you to Analytics Dojo SMC Private and to your contributions as a Data Analyst.

Yours sincerely,

For and on behalf of  
**Analytics Dojo SMC Private**

Name: **[BLANK – Authorised Signatory Name]**  
Title: **[BLANK – Authorised Signatory Title]**  
Date: **[BLANK]**


Acknowledged and Agreed:

I, **Sara Khan**, hereby accept the offer of employment from Analytics Dojo SMC Private on the terms and conditions set out in this Offer Letter and confirm that I have read and understood its contents.

Signature: ___________________________  
Name: Sara Khan  
Date: **[BLANK]**

---

ATTORNEY-REVIEW REMINDER: This draft is based on generic ADS fixed defaults and high-level Pakistan labour law considerations. A Pakistan-qualified employment lawyer must review and, where necessary, localise all provisions (including notice periods, probation, benefits, IP/NNN language, and dispute resolution) and confirm compliance with current federal and provincial labour laws and regulations before use.
